PD MEDICATIONS
RPD-LH, PD with prevalent left hemisphere damage; LPD-RH, PD with prevalent right hemisphere damage; H & Y, Modiﬁed Hoehn and Yahr (H&Y) Scale; UPDRS III, Uniﬁed Parkinson’s
Disease Rating Scale—motor part III; LEDD, levodopa equivalent dose; Sel, Selegiline; Ras, Rasagiline; DA, Dopamine-agonist; Rop, Ropinirolo; Pram, Pramipexole; Rot, Rotigotine;
(∧)ANOVA; (#)Chi Square statistic; and (*)Independent samples t-test.
MRI Acquisition and Data Analysis the IFG of both the hemispheres and the immediately adjacent
All 40 subjects received a structural MRI examination. MRI regions such as the rostral and caudal middle frontal gyrus
sessions were performed on a 1.5 T scanner (Siemens Magnetom (BA 46, BA 9, and 10), see Figure 1. Volumetric measurements
Avanto, Erlangen, Germany) and included [a] 3D T1 MPRAGE were also collected for three PD-related subcortical regions
scan (TR/TE = 1,900/3.37 ms, FoV = 192 × 256 mm2, voxel (Lewis et al., 2016): caudate, putamen, and globus pallidus,
size 1 mm isotropic, 176 axial slices), to perform cortical bilaterally using FreeSurfer subcortical segmentation streamline.
and subcortical measurements; [b] conventional anatomical Mean Surface Area (MSA) and Total Intracranial Volume (TIV)
sequences (PD-T2, FLAIR) in order to exclude patients showing was also computed for each subject and inserted as covariates

who presented with one or more macroscopic hyperintensities on well.
